It is proposed to exclude the installation of fire alarm speakers from the in-patient suite out of consideration for the patients well-being. The code requirements pertaining to audibility of fire alarms are to ensure that building occupants can evacuate the premises upon activation of an alarm. However, Building H is of B1 occupancy and patients cannot evacuate without being accompanied by trained personnel. It is paramount that fire alarm signals are audible for the building staff, but it is different for the patients as during the activation of the fire alarm system they cannot leave their suites, thus reducing the practicality of meeting audibility levels in patient suites brought forth by the 2024 OBC.

Plumbing  - The proposed Leslieville Station is divided into two buildings referred to as the North Building and South Building. The station buildings will beconstructed as an elevated guideway on an embankment adjacent to the existing GO corridor. The site is bounded by Queen, Degrassi andStrange Streets as well as the GO embankment. The station will be accessible from Queen Street, Degrassi Street and Strange Street. There isan existing Overhead Catenary System (OCS) infrastructure in place as part of the local Street Cars transportation network and the OCS islocated beneath the existing GO corridor and the Station Buildings.

Elevator shafts are permitted to penetrate the floor fire separations when a 1 hour fire separation are provided [Sentence 3.13.2.1.(8)]. The elevator shaft enclosures in the station pass through floor assemblies that are required fire separations. The portion of elevator shafts that interface with public circulation spaces is constructed of laminated tempered glass panes that are supported by steel construction. The configuration is not recognized as a system that provides a fire separation with 1 hour fire-resistance rating.The alternative solution is required to demonstrate a level of performance with respect to the attributed objectives and functional statements to the acceptable solution. An alternative solution is proposed to 2012 OBC Sentence 3.13.2.1.(8).The objective is to support the use of a laminated and tempered glass assembly for elevator shafts. The proposed analysis is to examine the thermal exposure from credible fire scenarios to determine the performance of the elevator shaft glazing system and demonstrate that it mitigates the spread of fire and smoke via the shaft.

The 2025 Proposal is a 37-storey tower with a total of 268 units and ~350m2 of ground floor non-residential GFA. Of the 268 units, over 50% of the units are larger two- and three-bedroom units and 30% of the total units are planned to be affordable.
